summary: Good matrix recording of a mixed‑quality show with a killer Scarlet>Victim>Fire jam and strong second‑set moments like Deal and West L.A.
review: The two fan reviews describe a solid 1991 performance that shines on the first‑set Scarlet>Victim>Fire sequence and an engaging second set featuring Deal, Samson, Stranger and a punchy West L.A. rendition. Recording quality isn’t discussed in detail but is presumed decent (matrix source), while the show suffers from occasional false starts and missed cues.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed‑to‑positive with several standout moments, and recording quality appears adequate; together they average to a solid four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit comments on audio clarity or mix; reviewers did not note any major flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The setlist is praised for its creativity, especially the second‑set sequence, though reviewers note hit‑or‑miss energy, occasional false starts and missed cues that affect overall flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Occasional missed cues and false starts noted on his part.
band_member_comments.Bob: Having fun and at times getting ahead of himself in classic "Bobby" style.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce's enthusiastic keyboard work highlighted, especially on Deal and West L.A.
